title: COMMUNITY CONNECTORS PROGRAM - 211info
text: ARC LANE COUNTY COMMUNITY CONNECTORS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Recruits and trains Direct Support Professionals (DSP) and then helps match them to children, youth, and adults who experience intellectual and developmental disabilities who may need support. The DSPs work one-on-one with children, youth, and adults on skill-building and inclusion services, both in the home and in the community. 4181 E Street Springfield, OR 97478 Eligibility: Individuals with intellectual or developmental disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 343-5256 107 , .. Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 343-5256 ext 107 Associate Director, Shoshanah Adams [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or email for more information FEES: Varies by service DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Lane County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Services: Home/Community Based Developmental Disabilities Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: EMPLOYMENT - EMPLOYABILITY PROGRAM FOOD - CARES FOOD PANTRY INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - FAMILIES CONNECTED/FAMILIAS EN CONEXION INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - STEPPING OUT REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - THE ARC OF LANE COUNTY SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - ESTEAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. ARC LANE COUNTY
